claim: Focusing on body does not remove mind but reallocates perceived capacities from rational agency to emotional experience.
Experiences define self through autobiographical integration while possessions remain external, explaining superior hedonic value.
Important secrets activate physical-burden metaphor, shifting perception and behavior as if person carries actual weight.
Recognition of contaminated knowledge triggers shift from intuitive to analytic judgment, improving accuracy for others without increasing latency.
Money primes shift beliefs toward free-market legitimacy and acceptance of inequality as fair market outcome.

phenomenon: overconfidence drives status through behavioral signaling
claim: Biased self-confidence produces behavioral displays that lead observers to perceive competence, conferring higher status independent of actual ability.
